concerns.text: Task T010 describes the random intercept model logic but fails to explicitly mandate 'cluster-level treatment assignment' in its description. FR-001 and US-01 Acceptance 2 strictly require treatment assignment at the cluster level (CRT design) to avoid pseudoreplication. Without this explicit constraint in the task, the implementation risks observation-level assignment, violating the core research design.

Task T018 implements the block permutation test but lacks an explicit constraint to 'permute treatment labels at the cluster level'. FR-004 and US-02 Acceptance 2 mandate that the null distribution must be constructed respecting cluster boundaries. Omitting this constraint in the task description risks implementing observation-level permutation, which invalidates the robustness claim.

Task T027 requires verifying runtime < 6 hours but does not include a verification step for the memory constraint (≤ 7 GB RAM). FR-006 and SC-003 explicitly mandate both time and memory limits. The task's verification scope is incomplete, failing to preserve the full constraint set.

Task T024 instructs implementing 95% CIs but does not specify the calculation method (e.g., Wilson score interval vs. normal approximation) required for binary error rate estimates. US-03 Acceptance 2 requires CIs for error rates; without a specified method, the implementation may use an invalid approximation for small sample sizes or extreme probabilities, violating the scientific rigor of the acceptance criteria.
